sentence: Followup-To: news.groups Approved: tale@uunet.uu.net, christian@aramis.rutgers.edu This is a RFD on a proposal for a newsgroup which would promote a sharing on the "Johannine hours" as proposed each month by the monks of the ecumenical community of Taize (pronounced te-zay) in France.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: NAME OF PROPOSED NEWSGROUP:
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: ========================== soc.religion.taize (Unmoderated) PURPOSE OF THE GROUP: ==================== The Taize Community is an international ecumenical community of monks based in France.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Many young adults come there to search for meaning in their life and to deepen their understanding of their faith through a sharing with others.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: This newsgroup will allow such a sharing through a monthly "Johannine Hour" which will be posted at the beginning of each month.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: A "Johannine hour" involves a short commentary on a given Bible passage, followed by some questions for reflection.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Any thoughts that may arise in consequence and that you wish to share with others can be posted here.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: We are not interested in theological debate, and even less in polemics.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: No expertise is required!
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The idea is to help one another to deepen our understanding of Scripture as it is related to our own life-journey.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The idea of "Johannine hours" was born in Taize as a simple response to all those who were trying to assimilate the Bible's message in the midst of their daily life.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Because of work or studies, it is often impossible to spend long hours in silence and reflection, but everyone can take an hour from time to time to enter a church, sit quietly at home or go out for a walk in the woods.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: There, in silence, we can meditate on a passage of Scripture to listen to the voice of Christ.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: During the time of silence, it is important to concentrate on what we understand and not waste time worrying if, in some Biblical expressions, we find it difficult to hear the voice of Christ.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The idea is to communicate to others what we have understood of Christ, not burdening them with our own hesitations but rather telling them what has brought us joy, what has led us to run the risk of trusting more deeply.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Perhaps those who read and think about the "Johannine Hours" in this newsgroup could share their reflections and discoveries with others.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The important thing is the complementarity between two aspects, the personal aspect of silent, personal reflection and the communal aspect of sharing, which through Usenet makes us a part of a worldwide network.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: BACKGROUND OF THE TAIZE COMMUNITY:
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: ================================= The following provides some background information on the life and vocation of the Taize (pronounced te-zay) community.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: "A PARABLE OF COMMUNION": August 1940, with Europe in the grip of World War II, Brother Roger, aged 25, set up home in the almost abandoned village of Taize, in Eastern France.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: His dream: to bring together a monastic community which would live out "a parable of community", a sign of reconciliation in the midst of the distress of the time.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Centering his life on prayer, he used his house to conceal refugees, especially Jews fleeing from the Nazi occupation.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: AN INTERNATIONAL AND ECUMENICAL COMMUNITY:
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Taize's founder spent the first two years alone.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Others joined him later and at Easter 1949, seven brothers committed themselves together to common life and celibacy.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Year by year, still others have entered the community, each one making a lifelong commitment after several years of preparation.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Today, there are 90 brothers, Catholics and from various Protestant backgrounds, from over twenty different countries.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Some of them are living in small groups in poor neighbourhoods in Asia, Africa, North and South America.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The brothers accept no donations or gifts for themselves, not even family inheritances, and the community holds no capital.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The brothers earn their living and share with others entirely through their own work.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: In 1966, Sisters of Saint Andrew, an international Catholic community founded 750 years ago, came to live in the neighbouring village, to share the responsibility of welcoming people in Taize.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: TAIZE AND THE YOUNG; THE INTERCONTINENTAL MEETINGS:
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Young adults, and less young, have been coming to Taize in ever greater numbers since 1957.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Hundreds of thousands of people from Europe and far beyond have thus been brought together in a common search.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Intercontinental meetings take place each week, Sunday to Sunday, throughout the year and they include youth from between 35 and 60 countries during any one week.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The meetings give each person the opportunity to explore the roots of their faith and to reflect on how to unite the inner life and human solidarity.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The meetings in summer can have up to 6,000 participants a week.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Three times every day, the brothers and everybody on the hill come together for common prayer in the Church of Reconciliation, built in 1962 when the village church became too small.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: "A PILGRIMAGE OF TRUST ON EARTH"
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: The community has never wanted to create a "movement" around itself.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Instead, people are called to commit themselves in their church at home, in their neighbourhood, their city or village.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: To support them in this, Taize has created what it calls "a pilgrimage of trust on earth".
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: At the end of each year, the pilgrimage has a "European meeting" which brings together tens of thousands of young adults from every part of Europe for several days in a major city.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: There have also been meetings in Asia and in the United States.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Every year, Brother Roger writes an open letter to the young.
label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Usually completed during a stay in one of the poor regions of the world, these are translated into thirty languages and provide themes for reflexion for the following year.
label: soc.religion.christian

label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: Discussion on the creation of this newsgroup will take place in news.groups.
label: soc.religion.christian

label: soc.religion.christian

sentence: EVIDENCE OF ADL SPY OPERATION SEIZED BY POLICE By Richard C. Paddock, Times staff writer SAN FRANCISCO -- Police on Thursday served search warrants on the Anti-Defamation League here and in Los Angeles, seizing evidence of a nationwide intelligence network accused of keeping files on more than 950 political groups, newspapers and labor unions and as many as 12,000 people.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Describing the spy operation in great detail, San Francisco authorities simultaneously released voluminous documents telling how operatives of the Anti-Defamation League searched through trash and infiltrated organizations to gather intelligence on Arab-American, right-wing and what they called "pinko" organizations.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Representatives of the Anti-Defamation League, a well-known organization in the U.S. Jewish community dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism, declined detailed comment Thursday but denied breaking any laws.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Police allege that the organization maintains undercover operatives to gather political intelligence in at least seven cities, including Los Angeles and San Francisco.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Groups that were the focus of the spy operation span the political spectrum, including such groups as the Ku Klux Klan, the White Aryan Resistance, Operation Rescue, Greenpeace, the National Assn. for the Advancement of Colored People, the United Farm Workers and the Jewish Defense League.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Also on the list were Mills College, the board of directors of San Francisco public television station KQED and the San Francisco Bay Guardian newspaper.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: People who were subjects of the spy operation included former Republican Rep. Pete McCloskey, jailed political extremist Lyndon H. LaRouche and Los Angeles Times foreign correspondent Scott Kraft, who is based in South Africa.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Authorities said much of the material collected by the groups was confidential information obtained illegally from law enforcement agencies.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: They also alleged that data on some individuals and organizations was sold separately to the South African government.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: In addition to allegations of obtaining confidential information from police, the Anti-Defamation League could face a total of 48 felony counts for not properly reporting the employment of its chief West Coast spy, Roy Bullock, according to the affidavit filed to justify the search warrant.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The Anti-Defamation League disguised payments to Bullock for more than 25 years by funneling $550 a week to Beverly Hills attorney Bruce I. Hochman, who then paid Bullock, according to the documents released in San Francisco.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Hochman, a former president of the Jewish Federation Council of Greater Los Angeles and one of the state's leading tax attorneys, will be out of the city until late next week and could not be reached for comment, his office said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Until 1990, Hochman, a former U.S. prosecutor, also was a member of a panel appointed by then-Sen. Pete Wilson to secretly make initial recommendations on new federal judges in California.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Hochman is a former regional president of the Anti-Defamation League.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The league, which initially cooperated with police, has denied repeatedly that its intelligence-gathering operation broke any laws.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: League officials will not confirm or deny whether Bullock was an employee and have said they simply traded information with police departments about people who might be involved in hate crimes.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But in an affidavit filed to obtain warrants for Thursday's searches, San Francisco police alleged that "ADL employees were apparently less than truthful" in providing information during an earlier search conducted without a warrant.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: David Lehrer, executive director of the Los Angeles ADL office, said the organization has not violated the law.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: "There is nothing nefarious about how we operate or what we have done," he said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: "Our record speaks for itself."
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The police affidavit contends that Lehrer had sole control of a secret fund used to pay for "fact-finding operations."
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Lehrer, according to the documents, signed checks from the account under the name L. Patterson.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: An ADL official said the account was used to pay for subscriptions to a wide variety of extremist publications that might balk at sending them directly to the Anti-Defamation League.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Bullock, 58, who has been collecting intelligence for the ADL for nearly 40 years, defended his efforts during a lengthy interview with San Francisco police.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He said that he gathered names from many sources and entered them into his computer under headings such as "Skins" and "Pinkos," but that did not necessarily mean that they were under surveillance.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: "I might never see or call up on 99% of them again," Bullock said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: "And it doesn't mean anything that they're in the files.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: It's not a threat to anyone's civil rights that a name appears in my files under, say, 'Pinko.'"
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: In recent years, Bullock worked closely with San Francisco Police Officer Tom Gerard, who fled to the Phillippines last fall after he was questioned by the FBI in the case.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: A former CIA employee, Gerard supplied Bullock with criminal records and Department of Motor Vehicles information such as home addresses, vehicle registration, physical characteristics and drivers license photographs.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Using files gathered for the Anti-Defamation League, Gerard and Bullock also provided information to the South African government, receiving $16,000 over four years, the documents show.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The file on Times staff writer Kraft, which was apparently sold to the South African government, provides some insight into the hit-and-miss nature of the spy operation.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The file notes that Kraft's articles "appear frequently in The Times and are well researched and written," but little else about the file is accurate.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The brief entry confuses The Times' Kraft with another Scott Kraft and provides the South African government with the wrong Kraft's physical description, photograph and other personal information.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Nevertheless, the documents provide illuminating details of how Bullock for decades infiltrated all manner of organizations, from skinheads to left-wing radicals, searching regularly through the trash of target groups.

sentence: But it would be to finance health care, it wouldn't be to replace some other tax that finances -- it wouldn't replace the income tax, for instance?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: There have been no discussions on that.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: In terms of getting a VAT tax through Congress, Senator Dole's press release today said VAT -- on tax day.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you think -- does it have a chance of getting through Congress?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Would it have a chance?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I have no idea.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Is that a consideration whether you all put it forward?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That would become a consideration if the President were to decide to do it.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: It's not in consideration now.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: You said at the beginning of the briefing that circumstances had changed and that had caused the VAT to now be under consideration.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes, what the President referred to this morning.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: These groups came forward and said this is something that has to be considered.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Those are the circumstances that have changed?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's the only difference between now and when he emphatically ruled it out that groups have asked it to be considered?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's what he said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Is that true?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Was there, in fact, some understanding that sin taxes would not produce enough money for the health care benefits?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I'm not going to get into the deliberations.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But, George -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: No.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What the consideration is, as the President said, groups came forward and said this is something you ought to consider.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The working groups are looking at it.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Is that the only thing that's changed since his prior statement and your prior statement on the VAT?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Can you explain how those groups -- how that information got to him that groups wanted it?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Was it just reading the newspaper or did groups make presentations?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think the groups -- as you know, the health care task force has met with dozens of groups.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But this is the President's knowledge that these groups had come forward.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think he was referring to what was coming to the working groups.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Obviously, there have also been published positions in the newspapers.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Have certain groups briefed him on the group's presentations to them?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know if they've briefed him --
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I mean, how detailed the briefings have been.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I know that the working groups decided to look into this after being pressed by these groups.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What kind of arguments did the groups make that were persuasive enough that the President would change the position that he had enunciated previously?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know, it's just they've had longstanding positions that this would be a good way to finance health care.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President wasn't aware of those longstanding positions?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He may have been at some level.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Obviously, he's been a governor for a long time and he knows the basic arguments for and against a VAT tax.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What we're trying to figure out here -- you're telling us that the only change, the only thing that affected this change in the President's attitude toward the VAT between February and now -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President's attitude hasn't necessarily changed.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I mean, he has not made a decision.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q I know, but the President said that it was off the table.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: So did you.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: And you're saying that the only thing that's changed is the positions of these groups, except you're also describing them as longstanding positions.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't see the change.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: If these groups haven't had any change in their position that's been made to the President -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Well, they've made the presentations to the health care task force.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: There's no relationship at all between the fact that sin taxes that he had said -- suggested in February that he favored will not produce enough revenue to finance -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't think he ever suggested that they would produce all the revenue.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Well, he suggested that he thought that those were appropriate ways to finance health care.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He did say that.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know that he said anything to refute that.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But, in fact, has the task force discovered that there wouldn't be enough revenue from those taxes to finance the kind of core benefits -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Again, I don't think that the task force ever suggested that there would.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q George, if he advocated a VAT tax, would that break his promise not to raise taxes on the middle class to pay for his programs?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I can't comment on a hypothetical situation.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But does that promise -- would that promise not to raise taxes on the middle class to pay for the programs prevent him from seeking a VAT tax?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President has made no decisions on the VAT tax.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: When he does, we'll tell you and we'll explain the implications then.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Which specific groups can you cite -- business, labor or otherwise -- whose recommendations to the health care task force has prompted this consideration?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't have the specific.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I just don't have that.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q George, can you tell us to what extend these other alternatives, for instance, the employer tax or the sin taxes or other financing options are also still on the table and what these options are?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: No, as members of the task force and representatives of the working groups have said, they are looking at a wide variety of options.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think that Ira Magaziner said that there are 20 different options under consideration.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But I'm not going to comment -- Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What's the scope -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I'm just not going to comment on them, no.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What's the scope of the need?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: How much are you talking about that has to be produced by one or a combination of the -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's what the health care task force is looking at.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Since there's not going to be any briefing on the Miyazawa visit, two questions: One, generally what does the President hope to use that meeting for, but more specifically, is his task complicated by the Japanese anger over the Vancouver note and the remark about market access at the press conference?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The Vancouver note?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Does no mean yes.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes and no. MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Well, I don't know.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I saw the Prime Minister's press conference where he was asked the question about that note and he gave a very gracious and complete answer when he was asked the question.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The questions of trade are something that certainly will be discussed between the Prime Minister and the President.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: There is obviously a trade imbalance between Japan and the U.S. that we want to do something about.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Also in those comments the Prime Minister made he suggested that the United States should come down heavy on him in terms of trade.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Are you going to oblige?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think the President will state our views on trade very clearly and our views on the trade deficit very clearly.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't necessarily want to agree with your characterization of the Prime Minister's comments.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q that we need specific export targets, specific numerical targets -- is that what he's going to discuss with Miyazawa?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: They're going to have a broad discussion of a wide range of trade issues.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't want to get into those specifics until after the meeting.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Why?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's the crux of the issue, right?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Whether or not -- does the President believe that without specific numerical targets, it is really, as he said in his press conference, sort of hopeless that this is going to change very much?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President believes that we must have pressure on Japan to turn the trade imbalance around.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I do not want to get into the specifics of how that would be done.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But does the President believe that their stimulus package announced yesterday will rectify the imbalance?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think, first, the President wants to get a full briefing on the stimulus package from Prime Minister Miyazawa himself, and then he'll make the comment on it.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q How about the Russian aid package?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: There seems to be some confusion about how the U.S. views that, Secretary Christopher saying -- or Bentsen saying the Japanese may need to do more, the Japanese saying that that's not what they heard?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Again, we're going to continue to work with all our allies in the G-7, and we're going to continue to press for help for Russian reform, Russian democratic reform.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: And I think that, so far, we had a very good announcement out of Tokyo and we're going to continue to work with our allies for bilateral packages.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you think the Japanese need to do more?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: We're going to continue to work with all our allies to do as much as we can.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Secretary Christopher was asked today on the Today Show this morning what he thought of Margaret Thatcher's comments on the Bosnia policy.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: And he said, "It's a rather emotional response."
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Over an emotional issue.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Right -- to an emotional problem.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Does the White House condone that kind of remark?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think that Secretary Christopher's remarks speaks for itself.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President believes also that this is a deeply troubling situation that we're trying to find answers for.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But that specific -- "rather emotional response" -- specific term?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: No, it speaks for itself.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: In connection with that, doesn't it seem that with the numbers of people who are being killed at this very moment, is it good American policy to put off some decisions that might be made now to help Boris Yeltsin win a referendum?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: For example?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: To take stronger action, to take military action -- air strikes, anything that can be done?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The President believes that what must be done now is to push harder for sanctions.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He is also -- as you know, the administration has been discussing lifting the arms embargo.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He believes those are the appropriate ways to increase pressure at this time.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What is your response to the critics who would say that the U.S. is now stymied by trying to help Boris Yeltsin retain the presidency?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: They're wrong.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: We're pressing hard for the Serbs to come to the negotiating table.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: We're pressing hard for increased sanctions, and we're talking to our allies about the arms embargo.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: You were putting great store in Vance and Owen getting people to agree to that.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Now, Vance and Owen have both said that military force to some extent would be acceptable.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Does that change your thinking?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Clearly, we're going to listen to whatever people who have put so much time into a situation have to say.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But at this point, the President is moving forward on sanctions and talking about the arms embargo.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q A follow-up on a Dee Dee comment this morning.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: She said she would be able to provide some administration officials who could document the effect the sanctions are having in Bosnia.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Are you going to be able to do that, or do you have anything -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't think that's what she said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's exactly what she said.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't think that's true.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But what she said -- we would look into the situation of what kind of evidence can be provided in Bosnia.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Obviously, if there are connections between the Bosnian Serbs and the Serbs in Belgrade and we are tightening the screws on the Serbs in Belgrade, that will have an effect over time.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I do not know day by day, minute by minute, what kind of help is being given between the two and what the exact effect has been.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But, clearly, we are slowing the shipment of goods into Belgrade.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: We are having an effect on the Serbs there.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: What kind of effect that will eventually have on the Bosnian Serbs I don't know.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But one thing I would say is if it were having no effect at all, I don't know why they'd be fighting it so much.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Are the First Lady's tax returns going to be released?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think there's a joint tax return.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: And it will be probably later today.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Is the President considering signing an executive order banning discrimination against homosexuals in the federal work force as part of the gay rights march here next week?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't think there's any proposal for that at this time, not that I know of.

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: It's something that the President promised during the campaign that he would do.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I have not seen any -- I don't think it's anything that's on his plate right now.

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know about on this issue.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I assume that he'll meet with representatives of the gay and lesbian community sometime soon, as he meets with representatives of lots of different groups and communities.

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you know if that's scheduled -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: It's probably going to be tomorrow.

sentence: Q Probably going to be tomorrow?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: (Laughter.)
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: It's a good thing you asked.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Who's probably going to be there?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: (Laughter.)
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q How long -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: That's all I know.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you know if it's at 3:00 p.m. tomorrow?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: (Laughter.)
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know what time it is.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't even know for sure if it's going to be tomorrow.

sentence: Q Environmental groups have asked him to make a major speech next week of some kind.

sentence: Is that going to happen, do you know?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't know if they've asked, but I think the President has always planned, as he did last year, to give a speech on Earth Day and I expect that he will.

sentence: If it's not exactly on Earth Day, it might be a day before or something like that.

label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Again, I don't know the specific timing of something like that, but it's certainly something under discussion and something we've been working on.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q Campaign finance reform?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: We're working on it.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you think it will be next week?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I'm just not sure.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The biodiversity treaty is something you're working on?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I missed the question.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes, something we're working on.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He asked if it was ready to be signed, and I said I didn't know anything about that but it's something we've certainly been working on.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you know what organizations might be represented in this meeting with the gay and lesbian groups?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I don't.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Do you know if he is going to reconsider being out of town on the day of the march?

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He's got to be at the Senate meeting in Jamestown, and I believe he's also going to be giving a speech to the American Association of Newspaper Publishers in Boston on Sunday, as he did last year.

sentence: Q Would you have told us if she had not pressed you on the question?

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: On what? Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: On the gays.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: When we went through the President's schedule for the day, certainly.

sentence: Q? George, what day is the publisher's speech?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Is that Sunday?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I think it's a Sunday.

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: And Saturday he'll be in Jamestown?

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Yes.

sentence: Q
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: So you're just going to be in Jamestown for one day?

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: No, I'm not sure.

sentence: I don't know how long the Senate thing goes.

sentence: It might go overnight.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I just don't know.

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: I'm certain if we had the meeting -- I don't know about open to the press, but we would have told you about it.

sentence: I didn't say it was going to be in the Oval Office.

sentence: (Laughter.)
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: But I didn't -- I'm not say that it's not, but I didn't say that it was.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: (Laughter.)
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Q at the White House in the Bush administration gay officials were invited to a bill signing ceremony and the White House had to repudiate having done that.

sentence: So I just wanted to make sure -- MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: Well, the President wouldn't do anything like that.

sentence: Q Certainly not.

sentence: Q

sentence: What marching orders did the President give to General Vessey?
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: MR. STEPHANOPOULOS:
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: They had a very good discussion for about half an hour today.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: He wanted -- the most important thing was he had a full accounting for American POWs and MIAs.
label: talk.politics.misc

sentence: The main legal principles of the Turkish State are summarized in Article 2 of the Constitution: "The Republic of Turkey is a democratic, secular and social State governed by the rule of law; bearing in mind the concepts of public peace, national solidarity and justice; respecting human rights; loyal to the nationalism of Ataturk, and based on the fundamental tenets set forth in the Preamble of the Constitution."
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Freedom of culture and religion prevailed during the Ottoman Empire, allowing the many nations and races within its boundaries to remain autonomous.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The fact that the Ottoman Empire was the longest lived in recent history may be attributed to these freedoms, despite the lack of any written Constitution.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The first attempts to create a written Constitution occurred in 1839 and 1856.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Although the documents adopted during these two attempts remained in force only temporarily, they provided the basic elements of a Constitution.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The 1876 Constitution was the first legal document to force a Parliament and the right of election to share the sovereignty of the Emperor.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The Constitution of 1906 placed some additional limitations on the Emperor, while increasing the power of the Parliament and the government.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The First World War (1914-1918) brought the Ottoman Empire to an end.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: By the occupation of Istanbul, the Parliament was dissolved and the Constitution was abolished.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The members of Parliament were sent to exile to an island by the occupying forces.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: During the Independence War, the "Turkish Grand National Assembly" held their first meeting on April 23, 1920 to serve as the legislative body of the new Turkish state.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: This assembly prepared the new legal structure of the Turkish Republic.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The new Republic was proclaimed on October 29, 1923 and the new Constitution was adopted in 1924.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: That Constitution served as the legal backbone of today's modern Turkish Republic.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: In 1945, Turkey adopted a multi-party political system.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The Constitution of 1924 was replaced by others in 1961 and 1982.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: All three Constitutions of the Republic have been based on the principles of parliamentary democracy, human rights, national sovereignty, division of powers, private ownership and secularization.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: "Major Principles of the Constitution" The constitution (with 177 Articles) establishes the structure of the Republic within the following principles: - The Turkish Republic is a democratic, secular and social state governed by law; - It should be governed to maintain public peace, national solidarity, justice, human rights and the objectives of Ataturk; - The language of the State is Turkish; - Sovereignty is vested in the nation without any conditions or restrictions.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Sovereignty is exercised by organizations authorized by the nation; - Legislative power is carried by the Parliament elected by the nation.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: This power cannot be delegated (transferred) to any one else; - Executive power is exercised by the President, and Council of Ministers; - Judicial power is exercised by the independent courts on behalf of the Turkish nation; - All individuals are equal, irrespective of language, race, religion, color, sex, or political beliefs; - Laws cannot be contradict those principles stated in the Constitution.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: "Structure of the State" In accordance with the Constitution, the structure of the state is based on the principle of "division of power" to create a balanced and self-controlled system.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The power is divided into "legislative power," "executive power," and "judicial power," balanced to secure freedoms and powers to control each other (self-control).
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: A. Legislative Power: The "Turkish Grand National Assembly" is a parliament with one House, elected by the nation for a term of five years to exercise legislative power on behalf of the nation.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The basic functions of this Assembly are: - to adopt, to amend, or to repeal laws; - to approve or to dismiss the Council of Ministers; - to supervise and to question Ministers or the Council of Ministers; - to debate, to amend and to approve annual budgets; - to ratify international agreements; - to grant amnesty or pardons.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Members of Parliament do not have any liability for their words (either oral or written) during the course of their legislative duties.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The country is divided into constituencies.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The number of representatives of each is calculated according to its population.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Every Turkish citizen over the age of twenty-one can vote.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Elections are supervised by the "Supreme Council of Elections," which solves all disputes or appeals.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: In each province, the local "Board of Election" runs and controls the election under the supervision and guidelines of the Supreme Council.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Members of the Council and Boards are elected among independent judges.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: B. Executive Power: The President of the Republic is the Head of State (not the head of government as in the Unites States).
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The main functions of the President are: - to represent the State and the Country; - to insure the implementation of the Constitution; - to coordinate legislative, judicial and executive functions; - act as the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces; - to ratify laws and government decrees.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The President is elected by the Grand National Assembly for a period of seven years.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The President may ratify or return the laws for a second debate, may call for a referendum.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Executive power is exercised by the "Council of Ministers," headed by the Prime Minister.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President from the members of Parliament.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The Prime Minister names the Ministers for approval by the President.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The new Government (Council of Ministers) reads their program at the Parliament and the vote of confidence follows.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: There are 21 (?)
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Ministers in the Council.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Ministers and other members of the administration can be sued in independent "administrative" courts for their misuse of power, administrative errors or functions against any law.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: C. Judicial Power: Judicial power is exercised by independent courts.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: No authority or power can instruct the judges or public prosecutors of the courts.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: These cannot be discharged, replaced or retired by executive authorities except for the reasons clearly stated by the appropriate laws.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: There are three categories of courts in the Turkish judiciary system: - Courts of justice deal with legal, commercial and criminal cases.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The decisions of these courts may be reviewed by the supreme court of justice upon the appeal of the parties involved.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: - The decisions or functions of the executive power (including the Prime Minister and Ministers or any governmental department) can be appealed in administrative courts if these functions or decisions are against the law.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: The decisions of these administrative courts may also be reviewed by the high administrative court.
label: talk.politics.mideast

sentence: Ref: Encyclopedia of Religion, Mircea Eliade MAGI: [Sneak Preview: Later still, eschatology and apocalyptics were a fertile meeting ground for Iranian and Judeo-Christian religions, as can be seen in the famous _Oracles of Hystaspes_, a work whose Iranian roots are undeniable and which most likely dates from the beginning of the Christian era, probably the second century CE (Widengren, 1968).

sentence: The Zoroastrian doctrine of the Savior of the Future (Saoshyant) was the basis for the story of the coming of the Magi to Bethlehem in the _Gospel of Matthew_ (2:1-12).]
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Old Persian word magu, rendered in Greek by magos, is of uncertain etymology.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: It may originally have meant "member of the tribe," as in the Avestan compound mogu-tbish ("hostile to a member of the tribe").
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This meaning would have been further resticted, among the Medes, to "member of the priestly tribe" and perhaps to "priest" (Benveniste, 1938; Boyce, 1982).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The term is probably of Median origin, given that Herodotus mentions the "Magoi" as one of the six tribes of the Medes.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: For a variety of reasons we can consider the Magi to have been members of a priestly tribe of Median origin in western Iran.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Among the Persians, they were responsible for liturgical functions, as well as for maintaining their knowledge of the holy and the occult.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Most likely, the supremacy of the Median priesthood in western Iran became established during the time of the Median monarchy that dominated the Persians from the end of the eighth century through the first half of the sixth century BCE until the revolt of Cyrus the Great (550 BCE).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Persians were indebted to the Medes for their political and civil institutions as well.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Even if hypotheses have been advanced concerning the existence of Magi of Persian origin in the Achaemenid period (Boyce, 1982), we must still maintain that they were of Median origin.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This is demonstrated by the eposide of the revolt of Gaumata the Magian, mentioned by Darius I (522-486 BCE) in the inscription at Bisutun (Iran), as well as by Greek sources.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Indeed, Herodotus insists on the idea of the usurpatory power of the Medes against the Persians through the conspiracy of the Magi.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The fact that the Magi may have been members of a tribe that handed down the sacerdotal arts in a hereditary fashion naturally did not exclude the possibility that some of them undertook secular prefessions.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This seems to be attested by the Elamite tablets at Persepolis.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: There is a theses, put forth by Giuseppe Messina, that denies that the Magi are members of an ethnic group by suggesting that they are simply members of the priesthood - a priesthood of purely Zoroastrian origin.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This thesis is untenable; on the other hand, the hypothesis that their name is related to the Avestan term magavan, derived from the Gathic maga (Vedic, magha, "gift"), is not without foundation (Mole, 1963).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The meaning of maga can probably be found, in conformity with the Pahlavi tradition, within the context of the concept of purity, or separation of the "mixture" of the two opposed principles of spirit and matter.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The maga, which has been erroneously interpreted as "chorus," from the root mangh, which is said to mean "sing the magic song" (Nyberg, 1966) and has been rendered simply by an expression like unio mystica, seems to be an ecstatic condition that opens the mind to spiritual vision.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: In any case, though there may be a relation between the Old Persian term magu and the Avestan terms magavan and maga, we must maintain a clear distinction between the Magi and the Avestan priesthood.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Avesta ignores the Median or Old Persian term, despite a recent hypothesis proposed by H.W. Bailey; Old Persian inscriptions ignore the Avestan term for "priest," athravan (Vedic, athravan), even if this is perhaps present in an Achaeminid setting in the Elamite tablets of Persepolis (Gershevitch, 1964).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The term magu has been present in Zoroastrianism throughout its history; the Pahlavi terms mogh-mard and mobad represent its continuation.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The latter in particular derives from an older form, magupati ("head of the Magi").
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: During the Sasanid period (third to seventh centuries CE), which saw the formation of a hierarchically organized church, the title mobadan mobad ("the high priest of high priests") came to be used to designate the summit of the ecclesiastical hierarchy.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Magi practiced consanguineous marriage, or khvaetvadatha (Av.; Pahl., khwedodah).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: They also performed a characteristic funeral rite: the exposure of the corpse to animals and vultures to remove the flesh and thereby cleanse it.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The corpse was not supposed to decompose, lest it be contaminated by the demons of putrefaction.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This practice later became typical of the entire Zoroastrian community and led to the rise of a complex funeral ritual in Iran and among the Parsis in India.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Stone towers, known as dakhmas, were built especially for this rite.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: During the time of Herodotus the practice of exposure of the corpse was in vogue only among the Magi; the Persians generally sprinkled the corpse with wax, then buried it.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The practice was widespread, however, among the peoples of Central Asia.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Magi were the technicians of and experts on worship: it was impossible to offer sacrifices without the presence of a Magus.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: During the performance of a ritual sacrifice, the Magus sang of the theogony (the Magi were possibly the custodians of a tradition of sacred poetry, but we know nothing about the relationship of this tradition to the various parts of the Avesta) and was called upon to interpret dreams and to divine the future.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Magi were also known for the practice of killing harmful, or "Ahrimanical," animals (khrafstra) such as snakes and ants.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: They dressed in the Median style, wearing pants, tunics, and coats with sleeves.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: They wore a characteristic head covering of felt (Gr. tiara) with strips on the sides that could be used to cover the nose and mouth during rituals to avoid contaminating consecrated objects with their breath (Boyce, 1982).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The color of these caps, in conformity with a tradition that is probably of Indo-European origin, according to Georges Dumezil, was that of the priesthood: white.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: In all likelihood, during the Achaemenid period the Magi were not in possession of a well-defined body of doctrine, and it is probable that they gradually adopted Zoroastrianism; they were most likely a clergy consisting of professional priests who were not tied to a rigid orthodoxy but were naturally inclined to eclecticism and syncretism.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Nonetheless, they must have been jealous guardians of the patrimony of Zorastrian traditions.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: By virtue of this they were the educators of the royal princes.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The wisest of them was responsible for teaching the prince the "magic of Zarathushtra, son of Horomazes" and thus the "cult of the gods."
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Magi who excelled in other virtues were entrusted with the education of the prince so that he would learn to be just, courageous, and master of himself.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: During the Achaemenid period the Magi maintained a position of great influence, although they were certainly subordinate to the emperor.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Despite several dramatic events such as the massacre they suffered after the death of Gaumata the Magian - in which, according to Herodotus (who calls himself Smerdis), the Persians killed a large number of Magi to avenge the usurpation - the Magi nevertheless managed to maintain their influence at court in Media, in Persia, and in the various regions of the empire where they were stationed as a consequence of the Persian civilian and military administration.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: No priesthood of antiquity was more famous than that of the Magi.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: They were renowned as followers of Zarathushtra (Zoroaster); as the teachers of some of the greatest Greek thinkers (Pythagoras, Democritus, Plato); as the wise men who arrived, guided by a star, at the manger of the newborn savior in Bethlehem; and as the propagators of a cult of the sun in India.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: But they were also known as the Chaldeans, the priesthood of Babylon, known for its occultism; this was perhaps the reason that the term magos had a pejorative sense in Greek, like "goes," "expert in the magic arts" (Bidez and Cumont, 1938).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Indeed, the Chaldeans were experts in all types of magical arts, especially astrology, and had a reputation for wisdom as well as knowledge.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: To understand the reasons for such various and sometimes discordant views, it is necessary to distinguish between the Magi of Iran proper and the so-called western Magi, who were later hellenized.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: In the Achaemenid period both must have been at least in part Zoroastrian, but the western Magi (those of the Iranian diaspora in Asia Minor, Syria, Mesopotamia, and Armenia), who came in contact with diverse religious traditions, must have, sooner or later and in varying degrees, been influenced by syncretic concepts.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Greeks were familiar with both kinds of Magi and, depending on their varying concerns, would emphasize one or the other aspect of them.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Classical historians and geographers, including Herodotus and Strabo, document their customs, while the philosophers dwell above all on their doctrines: dualism, belief in the hereafter, Magian cosmology and cosmogony, and their theology and eschatology.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Those sources most interested in the doctrines of the Magi even speak of Zarathushtra as a Magus.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: In doing so they are repeating what the Magi themselves said from the Median and Achaemenid periods, when they adopted Zoroastrianism.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: At that time they embraced Zarathushtra as one of their own and placed themselves under his venerable name.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Zoroastrianism had already undergone several profound transformations in the eastern community by the time of the Acheamenids and was already adapting those elements of the archaic religion that refused to die.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: It has been said quite often, in an attempt to characterize the precise role of the Magi in the Zoroastrian tradition, that the Vendidad (from vi-daevo-data, "the law-abjuring daivas"), part of the Avesta, should be attributed to them.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: (This collection of texts from various periods is primarily concerned with purificatory rules and practices.)
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Nonetheless, the hypothesis is hardly plausible, since the first chapter of the Vendidad - a list of sixteen lands created by Ahura Mazda, the supreme god of Zoroastrianism, but contaminated by an attack by Ahriman (Pahl.; Gathic-Avestan, Angra Mainyu), the other supreme god and the ultimate source of all evil and suffering - does not mention western Iran, Persia, or Media (the land of Ragha mentioned in the text cannot be Median Raghiana).
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Furthermore, it has been noted (Gershevitch, 1964) that if the authors had been Magi the absence of any reference to western Iranian institutions, including their own priesthood, would be very strange.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Magi were above all the means by which the Zoroastrian tradition and the corpus of the Avesta have been transmitted to us, from the second half of the first millennium BCE on.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: This has been their principal merit.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: We can attribute directly to the Magi the new formulation that Iranian dualism assumed, known to us especially from Greek sources and, in part, from the Pahlavi literature of the ninth and tenth centuries CE.

sentence: According to this formulation, the two poles of the dualism are no longer, as in the Gathas, Spenta Mainyu ("beneficent spirit") and Angra Mainyu ("hostile spirit") but Ahura Mazda himself and Angra Mainyu (Gershevitch, 1964).

sentence: This trans- formation was of immense consequence for the historical development of Zoro- astrianism and was most likely determined by the contact of the Magi with the Mesopotamian religious world.

sentence: In this new dualism - which was that later known to the Greeks (Aristotle, Eudemus of Rhodes, Theopompus, and others) - we can see the affirmation of a new current of thought within Zoroastrianism, to which we give the name Zurvanism.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: [See Zurvanism.]
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Thanks to their adherence to Zoroastrianism, the Magi played an enormously important role in the transmission of Zarathushtra's treachings, as well as in the definition of the new forms that these would assume historically.

sentence: Their natural propensity to eclecticism and syncretism also helped the diffusion of Zoroastrian ideas in the communities of the Iranian diaspora.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Greeks began to study their doctrines and to take an interest in them (Xanthus of Lydia, Hermodorus, Aristotle, Theopompus, Hermippus, Dinon), even writing treatises on the Persian religion, of which only the titles and a few fragments have survived.

sentence: In the Hellenistic period, the Magi were seen as a secular school of wisdom, and writings on magic, astrology, and alchemy were lent the authority of such prestigious names as Zarathushtra, Ostanes, and Hystaspes, forming an abundant apocryphal literature.

sentence: Later still, eschatology and apocalyptics were a fertile meeting ground for Iranian and Judeo-Christian religions, as can be seen in the famous _Oracles of Hystaspes_, a work whose Iranian roots are undeniable and which most likely dates from the beginning of the Christian era, probably the second century CE (Widengren, 1968).

label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The Avestan term saoshyant ("future benefactor"; MPers., soshans) designates the savior of the world, who will arrive at a future time to redeem humankind.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: The concept of the future savior is one of the fundamental notions of Zoro- astrianism, together with that of dualism; it appears as early as in the Gathas.
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: Zarathushtra (Zoroaster), as the prophet of the religion, is himself a Sao- shyant, one who performs his works for the Frashokereti, the end of the present state of the world, when existence will be "rehabilitated" and "made splendid."
label: talk.religion.misc

sentence: [See Frashokereti] Later Zoroastrian doctrine developed this notion into a true eschatological myth and expanded the number of Saoshyants from one to three.

sentence: All the saviors are born from the seed of Zarathushtra, which is preserved through the ages in Lake Kansaoya (identified with present-day Lake Helmand, in Seistan, Iran), protected by 99,999 fravashis, or guardian spirits.

sentence: The greatest of the awaited Saoshyants, the victorious Astvatereta ("he who embodies truth"), the son of the Vispataurvairi ("she who conquers all"), is the third, who will make existence splendid; he appears in Yashts 19.
